plotlines.name: Ned: Honor
plotlines.goal: uncover the truth about Jon Arryn's death and protect the realm
plotlines.obstacle: court intrigue, Lannister power, his own moral inflexibility
plotlines.stakes: his life, his family's safety, the stability of the realm

plotlines.name: Daenerys: Power
plotlines.goal: transform from powerless exile into a queen and reclaim the Iron Throne
plotlines.obstacle: her brother's control, Dothraki customs, assassination attempts
plotlines.stakes: her life, her unborn child, the Targaryen legacy

plotlines.name: Jon: Belonging
plotlines.goal: find his place and purpose at the Night's Watch
plotlines.obstacle: his privileged background, the reality of the Watch, divided loyalties
plotlines.stakes: his honor, his family's war, the threat beyond the Wall

plotlines.name: Tyrion: Survival
plotlines.goal: survive the accusations and political machinations against him
plotlines.obstacle: false accusations, family conflicts, enemies seeking his death
plotlines.stakes: his life, his freedom, his family's reputation

plotlines.name: Catelyn: Protection
plotlines.goal: protect her family from the Lannister threat
plotlines.obstacle: limited information, dangerous enemies, political complexity
plotlines.stakes: her children's lives, her husband's safety, House Stark's survival

plotlines.name: Cersei: Control
plotlines.goal: maintain power and protect her children's claim to the throne
plotlines.obstacle: Ned's investigation, Robert's bastards, political exposure
plotlines.stakes: her children's lives, her family's power, her own survival

plotlines.name: Robb: War
plotlines.goal: rescue his father and defend House Stark's honor
plotlines.obstacle: Lannister military power, political alliances, inexperience
plotlines.stakes: his father's life, the North's independence, his family's survival

plotlines.name: Arya: Identity
plotlines.goal: maintain her true self while learning to survive
plotlines.obstacle: court expectations, violence around her, forced disguise
plotlines.stakes: her life, her family connections, her sense of self

plotlines.name: Jon: Supernatural Threat
plotlines.goal: understand and defend against the supernatural threat beyond the Wall
plotlines.obstacle: disbelief, limited knowledge, isolation at the Wall
plotlines.stakes: the realm's survival, the Night's Watch's purpose

events.event: Three Night's Watch rangers discover wildlings slaughtered in ritual pattern
White Walker kills two rangers, Will flees south
Ned executes deserter Will for abandoning his post
Stark children find dead direwolf with pups, each claims one
Jon claims the albino runt and names it Ghost
Raven brings news of Jon Arryn's death and Robert's approach
King Robert arrives at Winterfell with the Lannisters
Robert visits Lyanna's crypt and asks Ned to be Hand
Robert proposes marriage between Joffrey and Sansa
Catelyn urges Ned to be cautious about Robert's offer
Viserys arranges Daenerys's marriage to Khal Drogo for an army
Daenerys submits to the marriage arrangement despite her terror
Jorah Mormont presents himself and offers Daenerys books at the wedding
Catelyn receives Lysa's letter accusing Lannisters of murdering Jon Arryn
Ned decides he must go south to investigate Jon Arryn's death
Bran discovers Cersei and Jaime in sexual embrace while climbing
Jaime pushes Bran from the tower to protect their secret
Maester Luwin tells Catelyn and Robb that Bran will never walk again if he lives
Catelyn refuses to leave Bran's bedside, neglecting Winterfell's affairs
Jaime expresses unease to Cersei about what Bran might remember
Cersei dismisses the risk, confident a child's word means nothing against theirs
Jon decides to join the Night's Watch and travels north with Tyrion
Jon gives Arya a small sword she names Needle before leaving
Jon says farewell to the unconscious Bran
Ned departs Winterfell for King's Landing with Sansa and Arya
Sansa and Joffrey encounter Arya sparring with Mycah by the Trident
Joffrey threatens Mycah with his sword
Nymeria bites Joffrey's arm in defense of Arya
Arya drives Nymeria away to save the wolf from punishment
Robert holds court and Cersei demands a direwolf be killed
Robert orders Sansa's innocent direwolf Lady executed instead of Nymeria
Ned carries out Lady's execution himself
The Hound hunts down and kills the butcher's boy Mycah
Daenerys struggles with painful nights with Drogo and grueling days of riding
Jorah rides alongside Daenerys and explains Dothraki customs
Daenerys learns from her handmaiden how to assert herself with Drogo
Daenerys faces Drogo as an equal, shifting their marriage dynamic
An assassin enters Bran's room with a Valyrian steel dagger to kill him
Catelyn fights the assassin bare-handed until Bran's direwolf kills him
Catelyn resolves to ride south secretly to warn Ned about the Lannisters
Jon Snow arrives at Castle Black and discovers most recruits are criminals and outcasts
Jon's superior combat training makes him dominant in practice, earning resentment from other recruits
Tyrion advises Jon to stop acting like a privileged lord's son and help the weaker boys
Jon begins helping the weaker recruits train
Ned arrives in King's Landing and takes his seat on the Small Council
Ned meets Littlefinger, Varys, and Grand Maester Pycelle on the Small Council
Ned learns the Crown owes millions to the Lannisters and Iron Bank due to Robert's reckless spending
Robert demands a lavish tournament in Ned's honor despite Ned's objections
Catelyn arrives in King's Landing in secret and Littlefinger arranges a meeting with Ned
Catelyn shows Ned the Valyrian steel dagger and Littlefinger identifies it as lost to Tyrion in a bet
Ned and Catelyn agree she must return north while he investigates from court
Arya chases cats and stumbles into the castle cellar where she overhears two shadowy figures
Arya overhears the men discussing Stark-Lannister tension and the need to delay war
Arya fails to convince her father of the significance of what she heard
Daenerys commands the khalasar to halt so she can explore on foot, defying Viserys
Viserys strikes Daenerys and her Dothraki guards intervene to humiliate him
Viserys realizes Daenerys is no longer under his control and the Dothraki respect her
Ned visits the blacksmith's apprentice Gendry, who bears a striking resemblance to young Robert Baratheon
Ned realizes Robert has bastard children throughout King's Landing, all with black hair
Ned obtains the genealogical history book that Jon Arryn was studying before his death
Tyrion arrives at Winterfell and gives Bran designs for a special saddle to let him ride despite his paralysis
Robb receives Tyrion coldly, suspicious of all Lannisters
Daenerys discovers she is pregnant with Drogo's child
Jorah tells Daenerys privately that a Targaryen heir changes the political calculation in Westeros
Daenerys announces her pregnancy to Drogo, who declares the child will be the Stallion Who Mounts the World
Viserys grows increasingly bitter that the Dothraki show him no deference
Ser Gregor Clegane kills Ser Hugh of the Vale during the tournament joust
Littlefinger tells Sansa the story of how Gregor burned his brother the Hound's face as a child
Ned suspects Ser Hugh's death was not accidental and that someone is eliminating Jon Arryn's witnesses
Catelyn recognizes Tyrion among travelers at the Crossroads Inn
Catelyn calls on her father's bannermen and arrests Tyrion for the attempted murder of Bran
Catelyn takes Tyrion east toward the Eyrie to avoid Lannister pursuit on the main road
Robert receives word that Daenerys is pregnant and orders her assassination
Ned argues against murdering Daenerys and her unborn child, calling it dishonorable
Robert refuses to relent on the assassination order
Ned resigns as Hand of the King in protest and announces he will return to Winterfell
Varys warns Ned that his investigation has made him a target and Jon Arryn was poisoned
Catelyn's party is ambushed by hill tribes on the high road to the Eyrie
Tyrion fights alongside his captors to survive the ambush
They reach the Eyrie where Lysa imprisons Tyrion in the sky cells
Tyrion discovers Lysa has already convicted him in her mind
Jon and his fellow recruits including Samwell prepare to take their vows at Castle Black
Jon is assigned to the stewards rather than rangers, which he considers an insult
Sam explains that the Lord Commander's steward is being groomed for command
Arya practices sword training with Syrio Forel in the fluid Braavosi style
Ned watches the lesson and his expression darkens, reminded that war is coming
Littlefinger takes Ned to a brothel where another of Robert's bastards lives
Ned realizes Joffrey, Myrcella, and Tommen are products of Cersei and Jaime's incest
Jaime confronts Ned in the streets with armed men after learning of Tyrion's arrest
Jaime's soldiers kill Ned's guards and Jaime wounds Ned before riding away
Ned wakes from his injuries to find Robert has returned from hunting
Robert demands Ned resume his duties as Hand, threatening to give the position to Jaime if he refuses
Ned reluctantly agrees to continue as Hand of the King
Robert rides out hunting again, leaving Ned to sit the Iron Throne
Villagers petition the court about Ser Gregor Clegane's raids in the Riverlands
Ned recognizes the raids as Lannister provocation to draw the Tullys into conflict
Ned sends Lord Beric Dondarrion to bring Gregor to justice and summons Tywin Lannister to court
Tyrion demands a trial by combat rather than face Lysa's biased judgment
Tyrion recruits the sellsword Bronn to fight as his champion
Bronn defeats Ser Vardis Egen using speed and cunning against armored strength
Lysa is forced to release Tyrion, who walks out free with Bronn
Viserys arrives drunk at a Dothraki feast and draws his sword in Vaes Dothrak
Viserys threatens Daenerys and demands the crown Drogo promised him
Drogo melts gold and pours it over Viserys's head, killing him
Daenerys watches her brother die without flinching and declares he was no dragon
Jon Snow and Sam take their vows before a heart tree beyond the Wall
Ghost emerges from the forest carrying a severed human hand
Ned studies the genealogy book and confirms all Baratheons have black hair
Ned realizes he holds the secret that could destroy the Lannisters but wrestles with how to use it
Ned confronts Cersei in the godswood, revealing he knows her children are Jaime's
Ned offers Cersei the chance to flee with her children before Robert returns
Cersei tells Ned that in the game of thrones, you win or you die
Robert is brought back mortally wounded by a boar during the hunt
Robert dictates his will to Ned, naming him Protector of the Realm
Ned writes 'rightful heir' instead of Joffrey's name in Robert's will
Robert dies
Ned sends a letter to Stannis informing him of the truth about Cersei's children
Ned asks Littlefinger to secure the City Watch's loyalty
Littlefinger advises Ned to make peace with the Lannisters and rule through Joffrey
Ned refuses to compromise with what he sees as a usurper
Renly offers Ned backing of his household guard to seize Joffrey
Ned refuses Renly's offer, unwilling to frighten a child with soldiers
Renly flees King's Landing
A wine merchant tries to poison Daenerys
Jorah Mormont intervenes and exposes the assassination plot
The assassination attempt enrages Drogo, who vows to conquer Westeros
Bodies of two dead rangers are brought inside Castle Black
The corpses reanimate as wights in the night
Jon fights a wight attacking the Lord Commander and saves him by setting it on fire
Ned enters the throne room to present Robert's will naming him Protector of the Realm
Cersei tears up the will and declares Joffrey king
On Littlefinger's signal, the gold cloaks turn on Ned's men, killing his household guard
Littlefinger puts a dagger to Ned's throat, telling him he warned not to trust him
Ned is arrested and imprisoned in the dungeons beneath the Red Keep
Sansa is kept in her chambers under Lannister guard
Syrio Forel fights the Lannister soldiers with a wooden practice sword, buying Arya time to flee
Syrio is overwhelmed and presumably killed
Arya recovers Needle and hides in the streets of King's Landing
Drogo's khalasar raids a village to fund the invasion of Westeros
Daenerys orders the Dothraki to stop raping the captive women, taking them under her protection
Mago challenges Daenerys's order, angering Drogo's warriors
Drogo fights Mago in single combat, killing him bare-handed despite taking a chest wound
Mirri Maz Duur offers to treat Drogo's wound, and Daenerys accepts her help
Lord Commander Mormont rewards Jon with Longclaw for saving his life from the wight
Jon receives word that his father has been arrested for treason and attempts to desert
Sam, Grenn, and Pyp convince Jon to honor his vow and stay at the Wall
Tyrion recruits the hill tribes by promising Tywin's gold and weapons
Tyrion arrives at Tywin's war camp where an army is assembling against the Riverlands
Robb Stark calls the northern bannermen to war and marches south with twenty thousand men to rescue his father
Catelyn negotiates passage at the Twins by promising Robb will marry one of Frey's daughters and Arya will marry one of Frey's sons
Robb splits his forces, sending a feint toward Tywin Lannister's main army while leading the bulk to Riverrun
The feint engages Tywin's forces in the Battle of the Green Fork, where Tyrion fights alongside hill tribes and is knocked unconscious
Tywin wins the battle but realizes too late that the main Stark army was not there
Robb's true force ambushes Jaime Lannister's army at the Battle of the Whispering Wood and wins decisively
Robb personally captures Jaime Lannister
Catelyn confronts the captive Jaime, who taunts her but cannot deny his vulnerability
Varys visits Ned in the dungeon and urges him to confess to treason for Sansa's sake
Ned agrees to lie for his daughter's sake, abandoning his principles
Ned is brought before the crowd at the Great Sept and publicly confesses to conspiring against the rightful king
Joffrey orders Ned's execution instead of showing mercy as expected
Ser Ilyn Payne beheads Ned Stark with his own sword Ice
Sansa screams as her father is executed
Yoren grabs Arya from the crowd, shields her eyes and pulls her away
Drogo's wound festers into a deadly infection
Daenerys desperately asks Mirri Maz Duur to use blood magic to save Drogo
Daenerys goes into premature labor and is carried into the tent where dark magic is being performed
Daenerys wakes to learn Drogo's mind is destroyed despite his body being saved
Daenerys discovers her son was stillborn and monstrous, the price for Drogo's life
Mirri reveals she acted deliberately as revenge, telling Daenerys she saved no one
Most of the khalasar abandons Daenerys for a new khal
Joffrey forces Sansa to look at Ned's head mounted on a spike
Sansa nearly pushes Joffrey from the rampart but the Hound stops her
The Hound quietly wipes blood from Sansa's lip after Joffrey has her struck
Arya travels north disguised as a boy with Yoren and Night's Watch recruits
Gendry joins the group, unknowingly fleeing Lannisters hunting Robert's bastards
Robb's bannermen debate their next move at the Twins
The northern lords reject serving any Baratheon king
Greatjon Umber declares Robb the King in the North and the lords kneel
Catelyn visits captive Jaime and strikes him for the Lannisters' actions
Mormont tells Jon his duty lies with the Watch, not the war of kings
Mormont announces a great ranging to find Benjen and investigate White Walkers
Jon rides out with the Night's Watch into the haunted forest
Daenerys builds a funeral pyre for Drogo and places her dragon eggs on it
Daenerys ties Mirri Maz Duur to the flames as a final sacrifice
Daenerys walks into the fire herself over Jorah's protests
Daenerys emerges unburned with three living dragons, her followers kneel
